# Recommended Briefing Agenda: Rachel Morrison

## TerraScale Energy | Full-Day Platinum Protocol Briefing

---

**Visitor:** Rachel Morrison, CTO, TerraScale Energy
**VVIP Tier:** Platinum (highest)
**Date:** TBD (URGENT: must occur before May board presentation)
**Location:** Raleigh Executive Briefing Center -- Premier Boardroom (mandatory for Platinum)
**Executive Sponsor:** Elena Vasquez, SVP Global Industries
**CEO Drop-in:** Required (15 min minimum, per Platinum protocol)
**Briefing Coordinator:** EBC Operations Team
**Briefing Classification:** Critical -- Champion Retention + $5M Deal Protection

---

### Agenda Overview

This is the highest-stakes briefing in the current cycle. Rachel Morrison is a Platinum VVIP, CAB Co-Chair, and our most valuable individual champion. A P1 production outage on March 28 has damaged her trust at the worst possible time -- weeks before she presents to her board to approve a $5M platform expansion.

The agenda is structured in three acts:

1. **Act I (Morning): Accountability** -- Address the P1 outage directly with engineering rigor
2. **Act II (Midday): Board Preparation** -- Help Rachel build the case for her board presentation
3. **Act III (Afternoon): Forward Vision** -- Demonstrate the grid modernization and edge computing roadmap

Every session must reflect the Platinum protocol standard and the technical depth Rachel expects. No junior presenters. No slide-heavy sessions. Whiteboard-ready environments throughout.

---

### Session Details

#### 8:00 AM -- Working Breakfast & Welcome (30 min)

**Format:** Working breakfast in Premier Boardroom
**Attendees:** Rachel Morrison, Elena Vasquez (Executive Sponsor), EBC Manager
**Catering:** Pescatarian breakfast, oat milk latte, sparkling water with lemon
**ALLERGY ALERT:** Tree nut allergy -- all catering must be nut-free. Verify with catering day-of.

**Objective:** Elena Vasquez sets the tone personally. This is not a standard welcome -- it is a direct acknowledgment from sponsor to champion that the P1 outage was unacceptable and that today is about earning back trust.

**Talking points for Elena Vasquez:**
1. Direct acknowledgment: "The March 28 outage was unacceptable, and I take personal responsibility for ensuring we make this right."
2. Frame the day: "The first session is a full technical RCA. We're not hiding behind summaries -- you'll see everything our engineering team found."
3. Board support: "We know your board presentation is in May. Part of today is ensuring you have everything you need to go into that room with confidence."
4. Personal commitment: "I'm here for the full day, not just a drop-in."

> **Note:** Rachel prefers 8:00 AM starts when she travels. She flies in the night before. Elena should be in the room before Rachel arrives -- Platinum protocol means the sponsor is waiting, not the visitor.

---

#### 8:30 AM -- P1 Outage RCA: Full Technical Review (75 min)

**Format:** Technical deep-dive. Architecture diagrams. Whiteboard. No slides.
**Presenters:** Principal Site Reliability Engineer + Principal Platform Architect
**Priority:** CRITICAL -- the most important session of the day and potentially the year

**Objective:** Present the complete root cause analysis for the March 28 production outage with the level of engineering rigor Rachel expects. She is a former Kafka committer -- she will evaluate the technical analysis on its merits. Anything superficial will backfire.

**Content structure:**

**Part 1 -- Incident Timeline (15 min):**
- Minute-by-minute timeline of the 4-hour outage
- Detection, escalation, response, and resolution sequence
- What worked in the incident response and what did not
- Communication timeline (when Rachel was notified, when Elena was notified)

**Part 2 -- Root Cause Analysis (30 min):**
- Detailed technical RCA: etcd quorum loss during certificate rotation
- Contributing factors: certificate rotation procedure, cluster configuration, monitoring gaps
- Architecture diagrams showing the failure propagation path
- Why existing safeguards did not prevent the outage
- Comparison with best-practice configurations

**Part 3 -- Remediation & Prevention (20 min):**
- Specific fixes implemented since March 28
- Certificate rotation procedure overhaul
- Monitoring and alerting improvements
- Preventive architecture changes for etcd resilience
- Validation testing results

**Part 4 -- Open Discussion (10 min):**
- Rachel and Anil Desai's team have been running their own internal RCA. Expect Rachel to compare findings and challenge any discrepancies.

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] RCA document: complete, peer-reviewed, technically rigorous (printed, bound)
- [ ] Architecture diagrams of the failure mode (whiteboard-ready, not slides)
- [ ] Remediation evidence: what has already been fixed, with test results
- [ ] Presenters must be principal-level engineers who worked the incident
- [ ] Pre-validate RCA findings with Anil Desai's team if possible (reduce surprise factor)

> **Critical warning:** Rachel quoted after the outage: "I've been your biggest advocate internally, and this makes me look bad." She needs to see that we understand the magnitude, not just the technical root cause. The RCA must convey that we understand what this cost her personally and professionally.

---

#### 9:45 AM -- Break (15 min)

Refresh beverages. Oat milk latte, sparkling water with lemon. Team check-in on RCA session reception.

---

#### 10:00 AM -- Platform Reliability Roadmap & Preventive Architecture (60 min)

**Format:** Forward-looking technical discussion. Whiteboard session.
**Presenter:** CTO Office -- Senior Platform Architect
**Tie to pipeline:** Directly supports confidence for $5M expansion and board presentation

**Objective:** Pivot from "what went wrong" to "how we ensure it never happens again." This session must convince Rachel that investing in platform expansion is a sound decision despite the outage. Present the reliability engineering roadmap as a systemic improvement, not a reactive patch.

**Content to cover:**
- Platform reliability engineering roadmap (next 12 months)
- Resilience architecture for mission-critical energy workloads
- Zero-downtime certificate rotation design
- Automated chaos engineering and failure injection testing
- SLA commitments and reliability guarantees for trading platforms
- How the reliability improvements apply to the grid modernization expansion
- Monitoring and observability enhancements

**Whiteboard exercise:**
- Collaborative session where Rachel and the architect design the target-state reliability architecture for TerraScale's expanded platform together

> **Approach:** Rachel loves whiteboard sessions. Let this session be collaborative, not presentational. She will engage deeply and the co-creation process builds confidence faster than any slide deck.

---

#### 11:00 AM -- Board Presentation Support: ROI & Executive Materials (45 min)

**Format:** Working session with materials review
**Presenters:** Account Strategy Lead + Industry Solutions Architect

**Objective:** Help Rachel build the business case for her May board presentation. The board will ask about the P1 outage. Rachel needs executive-ready materials that address the outage, demonstrate ROI, and make a compelling case for the $5M expansion despite the incident.

**Materials to prepare and review with Rachel:**
- Executive summary of the P1 RCA (board-friendly, 2 pages)
- Remediation and reliability roadmap summary (1 page)
- ROI analysis for the $5M grid modernization platform expansion
- TCO model: build vs. buy vs. alternative vendors
- Industry benchmarks: platform reliability in energy/utilities sector
- Customer reference data: comparable energy companies on our platform
- Risk analysis: what happens if TerraScale does NOT modernize the grid

**Deliverable:** Rachel leaves this session with a draft board presentation section she can use directly.

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] All materials drafted and reviewed internally before the briefing
- [ ] ROI model validated with real TerraScale deployment data
- [ ] Board summary documents formatted for executive presentation
- [ ] Print 3 copies of each document (Rachel, Elena, file copy)

> **This session is unique.** We are not presenting to Rachel -- we are helping her present to her board. The materials must be something she is proud to show. If they look like vendor marketing, she will not use them.

---

#### 11:45 AM -- CEO Drop-in (15 min)

**Format:** Brief executive-to-executive conversation
**Attendees:** Rachel Morrison, CEO, Elena Vasquez
**Protocol:** Platinum VVIP -- CEO drop-in required, 15 min minimum

**CEO talking points:**
1. Personal acknowledgment of the P1 outage and its impact on Rachel and TerraScale
2. Commitment to the partnership at the highest organizational level
3. Recognition of Rachel's extraordinary contributions as CAB Co-Chair
4. Confidence in the grid modernization vision and our role in supporting it
5. Invitation to a future strategic dinner or CEO advisory circle

**CEO prep requirements:**
- [ ] Brief CEO on Rachel's dossier, P1 context, and $5M deal status
- [ ] Prepare 3-4 specific talking points (printed card)
- [ ] CEO should know Rachel's name, title, company, and CAB role without prompting
- [ ] CEO should reference at least one specific contribution Rachel has made

> **This is not a formality.** Rachel needs to see that the relationship matters at the CEO level, especially after the P1 outage. A genuine, specific, informed conversation will carry more weight than any technical session.

---

#### 12:00 PM -- Working Lunch: Grid Modernization Architecture Deep-Dive (60 min)

**Format:** Working lunch with architecture discussion
**Catering:** Plated pescatarian lunch. NUT-FREE. Oat milk latte, sparkling water with lemon.
**Presenter:** Senior Solutions Architect -- Energy/Utilities domain
**Tie to pipeline:** $5M Grid Modernization (OPP-21502) -- the critical deal

**Objective:** Deep technical dive into the grid modernization platform architecture. Rachel will engage at the architecture level and likely challenge design decisions. This session should feel like a collaborative design review, not a vendor pitch.

**Content to cover:**
- Smart grid analytics platform architecture on OpenShift
- Real-time energy management data pipeline design
- Integration with TerraScale's existing AMQ Streams deployment
- Advanced Cluster Management for multi-site grid operations
- Data sovereignty and compliance for energy grid data (NERC CIP)
- Scalability model: from pilot region to nationwide deployment
- Architecture decisions: why OpenShift vs. alternatives for this workload

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Architecture diagrams tailored to TerraScale's specific environment
- [ ] Reference deployments from comparable energy companies
- [ ] Integration map with TerraScale's existing technology stack
- [ ] Have a whiteboard session prepared if Rachel wants to iterate on the design

---

#### 1:00 PM -- Hands-on Workshop: Grid Analytics on OpenShift AI (90 min)

**Format:** Interactive hands-on technical workshop
**Facilitator:** Senior Solutions Architect (must be someone who can match Rachel's technical depth)
**Setup:** Laptop stations, pre-configured environment, whiteboard

**Objective:** Rachel codes on weekends and has been known to open a laptop during briefings to test things. This session gives her a hands-on environment to explore the grid analytics platform capabilities. She rated her last briefing highly but wanted more hands-on time -- this delivers.

**Workshop modules:**
1. **Deploy grid analytics model** to OpenShift AI (20 min)
   - Pre-configured with energy-relevant sample data
   - Real-time sensor data simulation for grid monitoring
2. **Configure multi-cluster management** for grid operations (20 min)
   - Advanced Cluster Management policies for energy compliance
   - Demonstrate policy sync across simulated regional clusters
3. **AI model governance and monitoring** (20 min)
   - Model drift detection for grid prediction models
   - Audit logging and explainability for energy trading decisions
4. **Edge deployment simulation** (15 min)
   - Zero-touch provisioning for a simulated remote substation
   - Preview of the edge computing capabilities for the $1.8M opportunity
5. **Open exploration and architecture discussion** (15 min)
   - Rachel-driven exploration of any capability area
   - Whiteboard discussion of what she wants to see in production

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Pre-configure workshop environment with energy-specific data and models
- [ ] Test all exercises end-to-end 72 hours before briefing
- [ ] Ensure environment is robust enough for unscripted exploration
- [ ] Have backup environment and credentials ready
- [ ] Facilitator must be prepared for deep technical questions beyond the scripted exercises

> **Expect Rachel to go off-script.** Build the environment to support open exploration, not just guided exercises. If she finds something interesting, let her run with it.

---

#### 2:30 PM -- Break (15 min)

Refresh beverages. Assess energy level. If Rachel is energized by the workshop, maintain pace. If she needs a longer break, flex the afternoon schedule.

---

#### 2:45 PM -- Edge Computing for Remote Substations (45 min)

**Format:** Technical deep-dive with architecture discussion
**Presenter:** Edge Engineering Lead
**Tie to pipeline:** $1.8M Edge Computing opportunity (OPP-21688)

**Objective:** Deep dive into the edge computing architecture for TerraScale's 200+ remote substations. Rachel has compared this challenge across industries (healthcare, energy) and co-leads a cross-industry edge working group. She understands edge at a strategic and technical level.

**Content to cover:**
- Edge computing reference architecture for remote energy substations
- Zero-touch provisioning for unmanned, low-connectivity sites
- Ruggedized deployment models for harsh field environments
- Security model for edge nodes in NERC CIP environments
- Integration with the grid modernization platform (ties to $5M deal)
- Connectivity patterns: intermittent, satellite, mesh networking
- Cross-industry learnings (reference healthcare edge work with Sarah Chen)

**Preparation requirements:**
- [ ] Edge architecture diagrams specific to energy/utility substations
- [ ] Ruggedized hardware and deployment options documented
- [ ] Reference the cross-industry edge working group Rachel co-leads
- [ ] Prepare for Rachel to draw parallels across industries

---

#### 3:30 PM -- Strategic Roadmap: 3-Year Platform Partnership Vision (45 min)

**Format:** Open strategic discussion -- whiteboard
**Attendees:** Rachel Morrison, Elena Vasquez, CTO Office representative
**Presenter:** Elena Vasquez facilitates, CTO Office provides technical roadmap

**Objective:** Step back from individual products and projects to align on a 3-year platform partnership vision. Rachel thinks in terms of enterprise architecture and platform strategy, not individual product purchases. This session connects the dots between grid modernization, edge computing, AI, and platform reliability into a cohesive partnership narrative.

**Discussion framework:**
- TerraScale's 3-year technology vision and strategic imperatives
- How our platform roadmap aligns with TerraScale's vision
- Grid modernization: Phase 1 (analytics) to Phase 3 (autonomous grid operations)
- Edge computing: substations today, field operations tomorrow
- AI and ML: operational intelligence for energy trading and grid management
- Platform reliability: the investment roadmap that protects Rachel's advocacy
- Community and CAB: Rachel's continued leadership role and what we invest in return
- Sustainability and green computing (Rachel is sustainability-focused)

> **Approach:** Let Rachel paint the vision. She is a visionary CTO who thinks in 5-year arcs. Our role in this session is to show how we enable her vision, not to pitch our products.

---

**Format:** Structured close-out
**Presenters:** Elena Vasquez + Account Lead

- [ ] Confirm board-ready materials delivery (must be 2+ weeks before board date)

2. Board presentation materials: delivery date (2 weeks before board meeting)

4. Platform reliability improvements: milestone timeline

### Briefing Success Criteria

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| P1 outage addressed | RCA accepted as technically credible by Rachel |
| Board readiness | Rachel has materials she is confident presenting to her board |
| Champion confidence | Rachel reaffirms her internal advocacy for the $5M expansion |
| Pipeline protection | $5M Grid Modernization remains at Proposal stage (not regressed) |
| Relationship restoration | Trust trajectory stabilized; Champion status maintained |
| NPS Score | 9/10 or higher |
| Follow-up plan | All commitments documented with owners and dates before May board |
| CEO interaction | Genuine, specific, and memorable |

---

### Risk Factors for This Briefing

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| RCA is not complete by briefing date | Present progress transparently; commit to specific completion date. Never present incomplete work as finished. |
| Rachel has already lost confidence | Elena Vasquez opens with personal accountability; CEO reinforces at the highest level. |
| Board presentation concerns dominate all sessions | Dedicate a full session to board prep; weave board-relevant evidence into every other session. |
| Rachel tests the workshop environment and finds problems | Test environment 72 hours in advance; have backup ready; assign senior facilitator who can troubleshoot live. |
| The $5M deal is already in jeopardy with the board | Prepare ROI data so compelling that the outage becomes a manageable risk, not a deal-killer. |
| Frank Reeves (CISO) raises security concerns not addressed in the briefing | Prepare a certificate management and security remediation addendum; offer a dedicated follow-up session for Frank. |
